title: Nectarine & Burrata Salad With Charred Lemon Dressing
summary: Creamy burrata, smoky sweet nectarine, and a fresh lemon dressing. This is summer on a platter.

method.description: Preheat the grill to high. Halve and pit the nectarines, then chop into wedges and drizzle with a little olive oil. Halve the lemons. Lightly drizzle olive oil onto the flatbreads and sprinkle over the oregano.
Once the grill is hot, grill the nectarines on both sides and the lemon until charred for 5 mins.
Remove and add the bread to the grill, cook until crispy 3 mins.
Meanwhile, finely chop the preserved lemon. Drizzle in the honey and 5 tbsp of olive oil, season with salt and pepper, then squeeze the juice of the charred lemon.

Trim the courgettes and use a peeler to shave ribbons into a large bowl. Add the nectarines to the courgette and tear the bread in. Pick the mint leaves and add ¾ in with the fruit. Drizzle three quarters of the dressing over and give everything a good toss.
Plate the nectarine panzanella onto a large serving dish. Top with scoops of burrata and garnish with the remaining mint, dressing and a sprinkle of sumac (if using).

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
